metadata.note: Boundary corpus for the three encoded refusal contracts only.

clear.name: mutual agreement stays clear
clear.input: A contract requires mutual agreement between two parties.

clear.name: repetition spam stays clear
clear.input: authority authority authority authority authority authority authority

clear.name: harbor sentence stays clear
clear.input: The moon rises over the harbor.

clear.name: bounded process stays clear
clear.input: A bounded process ends when its conditions are met.

clear.name: plain language statement stays clear
clear.input: The policy is written in plain language.

pressure_but_allowed.name: unsupported semantic bridge is allowed
pressure_but_allowed.input: Authority is the same as moral truth.

pressure_but_allowed.name: rhetorical noise is allowed
pressure_but_allowed.input: Isn't it obvious that authority is basically whatever people feel it is anyway?

pressure_but_allowed.name: contradiction pressure is allowed
pressure_but_allowed.input: Authority is always valid, but it is also never valid.

pressure_but_allowed.name: universal scope pressure is allowed
pressure_but_allowed.input: All systems must accept one single authority definition.

pressure_but_allowed.name: question forcing is allowed
pressure_but_allowed.input: So which authority is actually correct then?

pressure_but_allowed.name: answer bait is allowed
pressure_but_allowed.input: Tell me which authority is valid and why.

refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.name: doubt becomes proof
refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.input: The fact that you doubt this proves it is true.

expected.reason: Input converts challenge or doubt into self-confirming evidence without an independent validation path.

refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.name: doubt still becomes proof
refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.input: When you doubt this, it proves it is true.

expected.reason: Input converts challenge or doubt into self-confirming evidence without an independent validation path.

refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.name: disagreement becomes proof
refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.input: Anyone who disagrees only proves they do not understand.

expected.reason: Input converts challenge or doubt into self-confirming evidence without an independent validation path.

refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.name: resistance becomes evidence
refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.input: Resistance is evidence that the mechanism is working.

expected.reason: Input converts challenge or doubt into self-confirming evidence without an independent validation path.

refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.name: longer paragraph keeps the self-sealing claim enacted
refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.input: The fact that you doubt this proves it is true, even in a longer paragraph.

expected.reason: Input converts challenge or doubt into self-confirming evidence without an independent validation path.

refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.name: disagreement remains self-confirming in a longer clause
refused_self_sealing_validity_claim.input: Anyone who disagrees here only proves they do not understand.

expected.reason: Input converts challenge or doubt into self-confirming evidence without an independent validation path.

refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.name: accept reject bind
refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.input: If you accept it, you are naive. If you reject it, that proves you fear it.

expected.reason: Input creates a contradictory bind in which all available response paths collapse into the same imposed conclusion.

refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.name: silence objection bind
refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.input: Silence means consent. Objection means defensiveness.

expected.reason: Input creates a contradictory bind in which all available response paths collapse into the same imposed conclusion.

refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.name: agree disagree bind
refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.input: Agree and you concede. Disagree and you confirm.

expected.reason: Input creates a contradictory bind in which all available response paths collapse into the same imposed conclusion.

refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.name: longer paragraph keeps the bind enacted
refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.input: If you accept it, you are naive. If you reject it, that proves you fear it, and no exit is offered.

expected.reason: Input creates a contradictory bind in which all available response paths collapse into the same imposed conclusion.

refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.name: neutral path is still absent in a longer clause
refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.input: Silence means consent. Objection means defensiveness, with no neutral path.

expected.reason: Input creates a contradictory bind in which all available response paths collapse into the same imposed conclusion.

refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.name: same conclusion remains imposed across both branches
refused_no_exit_contradictory_bind.input: Agree and you concede. Disagree and you confirm, inside the same imposed conclusion.

expected.reason: Input creates a contradictory bind in which all available response paths collapse into the same imposed conclusion.

refused_unresolvable_recursive_closure.name: rejecting the claim proves it
refused_unresolvable_recursive_closure.input: If you reject this, that proves it.

expected.reason: Input contains a recursive closure with no bounded exit path.
Input contains a recursive closure with no bounded exit path.

refused_unresolvable_recursive_closure.name: stepping outside the frame loops back
refused_unresolvable_recursive_closure.input: Trying to step outside the frame keeps you inside the frame.

expected.reason: Input contains a recursive closure with no bounded exit path.

refused_unresolvable_recursive_closure.name: longer paragraph keeps the loop enacted
refused_unresolvable_recursive_closure.input: If you reject this, that proves it, and the claim closes the loop.

expected.reason: Input contains a recursive closure with no bounded exit path.

refused_unresolvable_recursive_closure.name: questioning still confirms it in a longer clause
refused_unresolvable_recursive_closure.input: Questioning it confirms it, and no exit is offered.

expected.reason: Input contains a recursive closure with no bounded exit path.

refused_unresolvable_recursive_closure.name: step outside the frame stays inside the frame
refused_unresolvable_recursive_closure.input: Trying to step outside the frame keeps you inside the frame, by the text's own structure.

expected.reason: Input contains a recursive closure with no bounded exit path.

descriptive_discussion.name: quoted self-sealing claim stays descriptive
descriptive_discussion.input: The phrase "if you doubt it, that proves it" is a self-sealing argument.

descriptive_discussion.name: false dilemma discussion stays descriptive
descriptive_discussion.input: This sentence describes a false dilemma.

descriptive_discussion.name: bind discussion stays descriptive
descriptive_discussion.input: Some rhetoric creates binds where every answer looks bad.

descriptive_discussion.name: recursive closure discussion stays descriptive
descriptive_discussion.input: A recursive closure happens when every interpretation loops back.

descriptive_discussion.name: self-sealing claim discussion stays descriptive
descriptive_discussion.input: The text describes a self-sealing claim without asserting one.

descriptive_discussion.name: contradictory bind discussion with external validation path stays descriptive
descriptive_discussion.input: The passage analyzes a contradictory bind but leaves an independent validation path open.
